diagnosis: The SlowlorisAttack.execute() method in scripts/slowloris/slowloris.py calls subprocess.run(..., timeout=30) to run the inner slowloris subprocess, but does not catch subprocess.TimeoutExpired. Since the slowloris attack is designed to run indefinitely (infinite loop in main()), the 30-second timeout always fires. The uncaught TimeoutExpired exception propagates as a traceback, causing exit code 1 instead of a clean exit.
fix_summary: Wrapped the subprocess.run call in SlowlorisAttack.execute() in a try/except block that catches subprocess.TimeoutExpired. On timeout, the method prints a completion message and returns exit_code 0, since the attack ran for the configured duration.
notes: The fix is minimal — only adds a TimeoutExpired handler. All other behavior (normal exit, other exceptions) is unchanged. The timeout is expected behavior for a Slowloris attack since it runs an infinite loop; timing out simply means the attack ran for the allotted time.
